generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
// (None in document)

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorPositionEncoderLeft : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision position encoder for left motor axis with 0.06mm path repeatability and 0.1mm positional accuracy, providing real-time kinematic feedback for motion control"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorPositionDataLeft
]

instance MotorPositionEncoderRight : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision position encoder for right motor axis with 0.06mm path repeatability and 0.1mm positional accuracy, providing real-time kinematic feedback for motion control"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorPositionDataRight
]

instance MotorCurrentSensorLeft : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Current sensor for left motor axis capturing input current data for friction analysis and performance monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorCurrentDataLeft
]

instance MotorCurrentSensorRight : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Current sensor for right motor axis capturing input current data for friction analysis and performance monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorCurrentDataRight
]

instance TorqueSensorLeft : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Torque sensor for left motor axis measuring angular torque for friction identification and mechanical analysis"
    DTDFVocab:producedData TorqueDataLeft
]

instance TorqueSensorRight : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Torque sensor for right motor axis measuring angular torque for friction identification and mechanical analysis"
    DTDFVocab:producedData TorqueDataRight
]

instance AngularVelocitySensorLeft : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Angular velocity sensor for left motor axis capturing rotational speed data for motion analysis"
    DTDFVocab:producedData AngularVelocityDataLeft
]

instance AngularVelocitySensorRight : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Angular velocity sensor for right motor axis capturing rotational speed data for motion analysis"
    DTDFVocab:producedData AngularVelocityDataRight
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance MotorPositionDataLeft : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorPositionEncoderLeft
]

instance MotorPositionDataRight : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorPositionEncoderRight
]

instance MotorCurrentDataLeft : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorCurrentSensorLeft
]

instance MotorCurrentDataRight : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorCurrentSensorRight
]

instance TorqueDataLeft : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TorqueSensorLeft
]

instance TorqueDataRight : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TorqueSensorRight
]

instance AngularVelocityDataLeft : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom AngularVelocitySensorLeft
]

instance AngularVelocityDataRight : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom AngularVelocitySensorRight
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance FrictionBehaviorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "White-box analytical friction model developed using SysML IBDs and MontiArc Automaton for state-based behavior representation of mechanical components"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingEnabler, SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance MotionControlModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model representing serial robot motion control architecture using SysML Internal Block Diagrams with state machines for multi-axis coordination"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingEnabler, SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance GeometricCADModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "CAD-based geometric model of MABI MAX 100 robot providing spatial relationships between mechanical components for structural analysis"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CADIntegrationEnabler
]

instance ContextualDataTraces : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated and abstracted contextual data traces from physical system including position, current, torque, and velocity measurements transmitted via MQTT protocol"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorPositionDataLeft, MotorPositionDataRight, MotorCurrentDataLeft, MotorCurrentDataRight, TorqueDataLeft, TorqueDataRight, AngularVelocityDataLeft, AngularVelocityDataRight
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler, VisualizationEnabler
]

instance FrictionAnalysisData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed friction identification data derived from contextual traces, used for performance analysis and fault detection in mechanical components"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ContextualDataTraces
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler, VisualizationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLModelingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML v1.7 modeling environment used for functional and logical architecture modeling including Internal Block Diagrams and state machines for behavioral representation"
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionAnalysisService, MotionControlService
]

instance SimulinkSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink environment for detailed behavioral simulation of friction models and multi-physics system validation"
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionAnalysisService, MotionControlService
]

instance DataProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Software framework (implemented in C++/Java) for automated aggregation, abstraction, and preprocessing of contextual data traces"
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionAnalysisService, VisualizationService
]

instance VisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Dashboard visualization tool for passive monitoring of aggregated friction analysis data and contextual traces"
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

instance MQTTDataTransmissionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol implementation for real-time one-way data streaming from physical sensors to digital representation"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataIngestionService
]

instance CADIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "CAD tool integration for geometric model representation and spatial analysis within the digital twin environment"
    DTDFVocab:enables StructuralAnalysisService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataIngestionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated one-way data ingestion service collecting contextual traces from physical system via MQTT protocol for digital shadow representation"
    DTDFVocab:provides ContextualDataInsight
]

instance FrictionAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Passive friction identification service analyzing aggregated data traces to detect mechanical inefficiencies in motors and gearboxes"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionIdentificationInsight
]

instance MotionControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Behavioral simulation service validating motion control algorithms against physical system data using SysML and Simulink models"
    DTDFVocab:provides MotionValidationInsight
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dashboard visualization service providing passive monitoring of processed friction analysis data and contextual traces"
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizationInsight
]

instance StructuralAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Geometric and structural analysis service validating CAD models against physical system requirements"
    DTDFVocab:provides StructuralValidationInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance ContextualDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Aggregated contextual data traces from physical system providing raw input for analysis services"
]

instance FrictionIdentificationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identified friction patterns and inefficiencies in mechanical components derived from processed data traces"
]

instance MotionValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validation results comparing simulated motion control behavior against physical system performance"
]

instance VisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive dashboard views of processed friction analysis data enabling stakeholder analysis of mechanical performance"
]

instance StructuralValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validation results comparing CAD geometric models with physical system structural requirements"
]

// C18: Environment
instance ManufacturingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Operational environment for MABI MAX 100 serial robot within high-precision manufacturing domain"
    base:contains MABIMax100Robot
]

instance DigitalShadowSystem : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Digital Shadow implementation following Kritzinger et al. reference model with one-way data flow from physical system"
    base:contains FrictionBehaviorModel, MotionControlModel, GeometricCADModel, ContextualDataTraces, FrictionAnalysisData
]

instance MABIMax100Robot : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Physical serial robot system with six axes comprising motors, gearboxes, and controllers for high-precision motion control"
    base:contains LeftMotorComponent, RightMotorComponent, GearboxComponent, ControllerComponent
]

// C19: Components
instance LeftMotorComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Left axis motor component of MABI MAX 100 robot with integrated position encoder, current sensor, torque sensor, and angular velocity sensor"
    base:isContainedIn MABIMax100Robot
]

instance RightMotorComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Right axis motor component of MABI MAX 100 robot with integrated position encoder, current sensor, torque sensor, and angular velocity sensor"
    base:isContainedIn MABIMax100Robot
]

instance GearboxComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Mechanical gearbox component transmitting motion between motor and robotic axes with friction characteristics under analysis"
    base:isContainedIn MABIMax100Robot
]

instance ControllerComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Motion control system component processing sensor data and executing control algorithms for robotic operation"
    base:isContainedIn MABIMax100Robot
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in the described Digital Twin system is not explicitly detailed in the provided documents. However, the distinction between **digital shadows** (passive, one-way data flows) and **Digital Twins** (active, bidirectional) [32] implies that a DT system may include **control loops** where digital models generate commands for physical systems. If present, these interactions would likely involve:
- **Command types**: High-level control signals (e.g., adjustment parameters for CNC machines [57]), predictive maintenance actions, or operational optimizations.
- **Control message formats**: Structured payloads (e.g., JSON or XML) transmitted via protocols like **MQTT** ([52]) or **OPC UA**, though the latter is not mentioned in the documents.
- **Validation and authentication**: Mechanisms to ensure command integrity (e.g., digital signatures, role-based access control), though specifics are absent.
- **Feedback systems**: Closed-loop validation where physical responses (e.g., sensor feedback) are fed back into the digital model for iterative refinement.
- **Conflict resolution**: Not addressed in the documents, but may rely on **priority-based rules** or **human-in-the-loop** interventions for critical operations.
- **Command execution confirmation**: Acknowledgment mechanisms (e.g., timestamps, checksums) to verify successful transmission and execution, though not detailed.

Since the documents emphasize **digital shadows** (passive) and do not describe active control interactions, this characteristic is **not substantiated by the provided context**.
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system described in the documents does not explicitly specify synchronization frequencies (e.g., Hz, ms) or latency requirements between physical and digital components. However, the system leverages **data traceability** and **transitive tracking** via 'originates from association' to ensure semantic consistency and meaningful linking of real-time, data-driven representations. While the text mentions 'live measurements' and 'data traces' for digital shadows, no concrete temporal granularity (e.g., sampling rates, update intervals) or latency-sensitive operations are detailed. The system relies on **processing components** (e.g., simulation or data calculation models in tools like MathWorks Simulink or C++/Java) to compute higher-level abstractions from input data traces, but no explicit time-scale constraints (e.g., real-time vs. near-real-time) are provided."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system’s orchestration follows a **reference model-based approach**, where the **Digital Shadow Reference Model** defines core concepts (e.g., data traces, processing components, metadata) that are **concretized** into a **conforming model** for specific use cases. The **architecture pattern** relies on **component-based integration**, where **Processing components** (implemented via simulation tools like Simulink or general-purpose languages like C++/Java) execute data calculation or simulation logic. **Resource management** is implicit in the traceability system, ensuring data traces are linked to their computational origins (e.g., models, tools). System-wide coordination is achieved through **association-based traceability**, enabling transitive tracking of data flows and ensuring consistency across hierarchical layers (e.g., requirements → functions → physics). No explicit orchestration frameworks (e.g., Kubernetes, microservices) are mentioned."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) system is structured through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, encompassing multiple abstraction layers: **requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and product/technical layers**. These stages are governed by methodologies such as **RFLP (Requirements, Functions, Logics, Physics)**, **OOSEM (Object-Oriented Systems Engineering Methodology)**, **SPES (System and Product Engineering System)**, and **SMArDT (Smart Digital Twin Methodology)**. The lifecycle transitions between these stages involve:
  - **Requirements Phase**: Defining system objectives and constraints using structured modeling languages.
  - **Functional/Logical Architecture Phase**: Describing system components, interfaces, and connectors via **architecture description languages (ADLs)** like **ACME, AADL (Avionics System Architecture Language), MontiArc, or SysML Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)**. Behavior is modeled using **state machines (SysML v1.7)** or **MontiArc Automaton**.
  - **Product/Technical Layer**: Integrating detailed physical models with simulation tools (e.g., **MathWorks Simulink**) or general-purpose programming languages (e.g., **C++, Java**).
Lifecycle governance is implicit in MBSE frameworks, ensuring traceability across heterogeneous models and tools, with **version control** and **transition management** critical for maintaining consistency. The lifecycle also integrates **heterogeneous stakeholder perspectives** (e.g., engineers, operators) via collaborative modeling environments.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system addresses **fidelity and validity** through:
- **Model Fidelity Requirements**: Achieved via **multi-layered MBSE methodologies** (e.g., RFLP, OOSEM) that ensure **logical consistency** across requirements, functional, and physical layers. **Architecture Description Languages (ADLs)** like **AADL or MontiArc** enforce structural fidelity by defining **component interfaces, connectors, and composition rules**.
- **Validation and Verification (V&V) Methods**: Implicitly supported by:
  - **Toolchain Integration**: Combining **SysML (for architecture), Simulink (for behavior), and general-purpose code (C++/Java)** to validate model behavior against physical expectations.
  - **State Machines (SysML v1.7)**: Used to verify behavioral fidelity in discrete-event systems.
- **Uncertainty Sources and Quantification**: Not explicitly detailed, but **data aggregation from Digital Shadows** introduces **contextual noise** (e.g., sensor inaccuracies, sampling delays). Mitigation relies on **abstraction layers** (e.g., UML models) to filter raw data.
- **Error Detection and Correction**: **Active DT components** (e.g., simulation engines) can detect anomalies via **comparative analysis** (e.g., predicted vs. actual states), though specific mechanisms (e.g., machine learning, rule-based systems) are not mentioned.
- **Quality Assurance Processes**: Enforced through **MBSE best practices**, including:
  - **Stakeholder Integration**: Ensuring alignment across **heterogeneous perspectives** (e.g., engineers, operators).
  - **Model Interoperability**: Using **standardized notations** (SysML, ADLs) to maintain consistency across tools.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical connection for this system is limited to the **MQTT protocol** for streaming sensor data from the physical system to the digital shadow. No details on network topology, bandwidth, latency, or redundancy mechanisms are provided. The documents do not specify hardware controllers, cloud/edge infrastructure, or security protocols. The system is described as a **dashboard-based visualization** with live-data linking, but no explicit technical architecture (e.g., hybrid, cloud, or edge) is mentioned. The only concrete reference is the use of MQTT for data transmission, with no further elaboration on communication infrastructure."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Horizontal integration in the described Digital Twin system is facilitated through **heterogeneous modeling notations and tools**, including **architecture description languages (ADLs)** like SysML, AADL, or MontiArc, and **simulation tools** (e.g., Simulink). **Data exchange protocols** are not explicitly detailed, but the system assumes **interoperability standards** via **conforming models** that map reference concepts to concrete elements (e.g., class diagrams with associations). Dependencies on external systems are implied through **purpose-driven data trace collection** from cyber-physical systems (CPS), where external stakeholders (e.g., enterprise systems) may provide input data traces. Integration patterns are **tool-specific** (e.g., SysML for architecture, Simulink for behavior) and **use-case-specific**, with no mention of standardized APIs or middleware (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT)."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system adheres to **industry-standard modeling languages and tools** without explicit compliance frameworks or certification requirements. Key standards include:
- **SysML (Systems Modeling Language)** for MBSE and architecture modeling,
- **AADL (Architecture Analysis & Design Language)** for component-connector-based system descriptions,
- **MontiArc** for architecture and behavioral modeling (including automaton-based state machines),
- **MathWorks Simulink** for dynamic system simulation and integration with live data.
No interoperability standards (e.g., OPC UA, ISO/IEC 15288, SAE AS5506, or IEC 61499) are mentioned. Compliance is implicitly tied to the **adoption of these tools/languages** as per MBSE best practices, but no formal certification or framework adherence is documented.
"
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system described in the provided documents incorporates **safety-critical measures** primarily through **physical safety mechanisms** rather than explicit cybersecurity protocols. Specifically, the **safety cell** (as referenced in [45]) restricts the operational workspace of robotic systems (e.g., the MABI robotic arm) to predefined boundaries, ensuring collision avoidance and operator safety. This is achieved via **hardware-based safety barriers** and **motion control constraints**, but no details on cybersecurity frameworks (e.g., IEC 62443, OAuth 2.0, or encryption protocols) are provided. Additionally, **friction modeling for structured learning of robot dynamics** ([45]) enhances motion accuracy, indirectly contributing to safety by reducing unintended deviations, but this remains a **physical/simulation-based approach** rather than a cybersecurity measure. No explicit mention of **access control mechanisms**, **authentication systems**, or **threat mitigation strategies** (e.g., firewalls, intrusion detection) is found in the documents. The system’s safety focus aligns with **ISO 13849** (safety-related parts of control systems) rather than cybersecurity standards like ISO 27001 or NIST SP 800-53."
]
